Petition Of Alicia M. Gamez For Order Directing Payment For Attorney Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Aug-24-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alicia Gamez, via Zoom, in propria persona, Petitioner.

The court acknowledges the joinder filed by the trustee in this petition and advises that is satisfied with the requests set forth in the petition and confirms that Ms. Gamez has nothing additional to add.

The Petition for Order Directing Payment for Attorney Fees and Costs is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

Off Calendar Apr-08-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Deb Fox, via Zoom, for Hiro Nagamine, via Zoom, Trustee; Caroline Hinshaw, via Zoom, former court-appointed attorney for Kazuko Miyamoto, Petitioner; Alicia Gamez, via Zoom, Interested Party.

The court confirms with Ms. Hinshaw that she withdraws her request for a lien and offers oral testimony in support of her request for fees. The court, Ms. Fox, and Ms. Gamez discuss the Yorozu Law Group's request for fees and notes its concerns with the number of attorneys involved in this and related conservatorship matters. The court and the parties discuss the Gordon-Creed, Kelley, Holl and Sugerman request. The bench officer discloses that Kevin Holl, a partner at the previously mentioned firm, is the spouse of Judge Kelley, a SF Superior Court Judge, and that both are friends of his.

After additional discussion, both matters on today's calendar are taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Glisson. Not reported.

Off Calendar Dec-14-2021 Continued To Jan-11-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Deb Fox, via ZOOM, for Hiro Nagamine, Trustee.

Ms. Fox reports on why the accounting has been delayed and advises that she should have an accounting filed by next week. Ms. Fox reports that she intended to file a supplemental Inventory and Appraisal ("I&A") but has not yet done so because the value of several assets remain unclear. The court advises Ms. Fox that she should consider filing the I&A even if values of assets cannot be determined, to keep the court updated. Ms. Fox advises that she discuss the preceding with the probate referee and file a supplemental I&A soon.

The Status of Accounting is continued to Jan-11-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status on Filing of Supplemental Inventory and Appraisal is added for Feb-15-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.
